mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.26% represents a restrictive monetary stance. Sustained elevated FEDFUNDS above 5.0% signals continued credit tightening that depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.6% reflects a tight labor market near full employment. When UNRATE remains below 5.0% for consecutive months, wage pressures and inflation expectations rise, forcing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy. This persistent tightness predicts negative equity returns as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 5.0% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE level and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1467K units and retail sales (RSAFS) at 376B dollars jointly signal demand momentum. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, consumer and residential investment strength supports corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1467K and RSAFS at 376B as of June 2007
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances, or joint signal produces lower Rank IC than either series alone over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 113.86 reflects supply-side capacity utilization. A decline in INDPRO below its 3-month moving average signals weakening manufacturing activity and potential recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines below its 3-month MA, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 138,122K and unemployment rate (UNRATE) at 4.6% jointly indicate labor market tightness. When PAYEMS growth slows to below 100K month-over-month while UNRATE remains stable or rises, this signals deteriorating labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth falls below 100K while UNRATE rises or remains above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138122K and UNRATE at 4.6% as of July 2007
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal produces lower predictive power than PAYEMS growth alone over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) at 208.03 combined with elevated FEDFUNDS at 5.26% signals the Fed is in active inflation-fighting mode. When CPI growth accelerates month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%, the Fed's commitment to further tightening depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3% and FEDFUNDS > 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 208.03 and FEDFUNDS at 5.26% as of July 2007
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a regression controlling for individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 11507.9B dollars represents the broadest demand measure. Quarterly GDP growth below 2.0% annualized signals economic deceleration that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when GDP growth is below 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 1467K units represent forward-looking residential investment demand. A sustained decline in HOUST below 1400K for two consecutive months signals housing market weakness that cascades into reduced construction employment and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1400K for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
